title: Access Control — Role-Based Violations (Coder Accessing Medication Admin)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
HIPAA Security Rule §164.308(a)(4) Information access management — UC detects role-based access violations (e.g. coders accessing medication admin), the direct information-access-management evidence.

spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" earliest=-24h
| lookup epic_user_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT hr_role, allowed_activities mvsep="|"
| eval allowed=if(isnull(allowed_activities), "*", allowed_activities)
| where isnotnull(hr_role) AND NOT match(Activity, allowed)
| stats count values(Activity) as bad_activities by USER_ID, hr_role
| sort - count
value: Detects EHR workspace or activity codes inconsistent with HR job role, supporting least-privilege access control for ePHI workflows.
implementation: (1) Refresh role mapping nightly from IAM; (2) tune with clinical float pools; (3) integrate with provisioning QA before go-live.
visualization: Table (user, role, violations), Heatmap (activity vs role).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
